{{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = [[Hachiman]] |image=YamagatakenNishimurayamagun-YachiHachimangu-01.jpg}} The Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine is a historic shrine located in 224 Yachi, Kahoku Town, Nishimurayama County, Yamagata Prefecture<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Japan Heritage Site Yamadera and Safflower Experience » Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine |url=https://yamadera-benibana.jp/experience/en/benibana/%E8%B0%B7%E5%9C%B0%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE/ |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=yamadera-benibana.jp}}</ref>. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]<ref name="bunpu">{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine Names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}}</ref><!--I think this is sufficient citation but it is just about interpreting the name-->
It was established in 1091 by the renowned samurai commander Minamoto No Yoshiie, also known as Hachimantaro. The shrine has a rich history of preserving the Hayashike Bugaku court dance and music, which is recognized as a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. <ref name=":0" />This tradition has been passed down through generations of the Hayashi family priests for over 1,100 years<ref name=":0" />.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Yamagata Pref. Festival & Event｜TOHOKU Cruise Promotion and Cooperation Committee |url=https://www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp/tohoku_cruise/en/020/020/060/ev_yamagata.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp}}</ref> the priests of the shrine come from that family.<ref name=":1" />
== History == The bugaku costumes used in the Hayashike Bugaku performances are distinctively designed with bright crimson cloth, dyed using the benizome method. The surrounding Rokku district was historically inhabited by six families whose members performed alongside the Hayashi family. Descendants of these families continue to be responsible for Hayashike Bugaku performances to this day.<ref name=":0" /> The traditional dance is done on September 14-15 every year.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Benibana Museum (The Safflower Museum) |url=https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/database/benibana/bunken/book/bmuseum/bmuseum3.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp}}</ref>
== festivals == The main festival of the shrine is Donga Matsuri, which is the largest festival in the region. It features a procession with a portable shrine extending up to 2 km in length. Additionally, the festival showcases Yachi Yakko performers and Hayashi Yatai floats with musicians playing festive tunes. An essential part of the Donga Matsuri is the Yayu no bugaku, performed around bonfires at night, providing participants with a solemn atmosphere and a reflection on the eternal flow of time.<ref name=":0" />
Throughout the year, the shrine hosts various other seasonal events. These include the New Year’s Day Festival and Hatsumode on January 1, the Kiuri Tenno-sai and Chigaya-nowa-kuguri festivals in summer, and the Shichi-Go-San festival in autumn. These events hold significant spiritual importance for the local community.<ref name=":0" />

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original at [[every:Yachi Hachimangu]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 12 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = [[Hachiman]] |image=YamagatakenNishimurayamagun-YachiHachimangu-01.jpg}} The Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine is a historic shrine located in 224 Yachi, Kahoku Town, Nishimurayama County, Yamagata Prefecture<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Japan Heritage Site Yamadera and Safflower Experience » Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine |url=https://yamadera-benibana.jp/experience/en/benibana/%E8%B0%B7%E5%9C%B0%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE/ |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=yamadera-benibana.jp}}</ref>. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]<ref name="bunpu">{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine Names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}}</ref><!--I think this is sufficient citation but it is just about interpreting the name-->
It was established in 1091 by the renowned samurai commander Minamoto No Yoshiie, also known as Hachimantaro. The shrine has a rich history of preserving the Hayashike Bugaku court dance and music, which is recognized as a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. <ref name=":0" />This tradition has been passed down through generations of the Hayashi family priests for over 1,100 years<ref name=":0" />.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Yamagata Pref. Festival & Event｜TOHOKU Cruise Promotion and Cooperation Committee |url=https://www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp/tohoku_cruise/en/020/020/060/ev_yamagata.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp}}</ref> the priests of the shrine come from that family.<ref name=":1" />
== History == The bugaku costumes used in the Hayashike Bugaku performances are distinctively designed with bright crimson cloth, dyed using the benizome method. The surrounding Rokku district was historically inhabited by six families whose members performed alongside the Hayashi family. Descendants of these families continue to be responsible for Hayashike Bugaku performances to this day.<ref name=":0" /> The traditional dance is done on September 14-15 every year.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Benibana Museum (The Safflower Museum) |url=https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/database/benibana/bunken/book/bmuseum/bmuseum3.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp}}</ref>
== festivals == The main festival of the shrine is Donga Matsuri, which is the largest festival in the region. It features a procession with a portable shrine extending up to 2 km in length. Additionally, the festival showcases Yachi Yakko performers and Hayashi Yatai floats with musicians playing festive tunes. An essential part of the Donga Matsuri is the Yayu no bugaku, performed around bonfires at night, providing participants with a solemn atmosphere and a reflection on the eternal flow of time.<ref name=":0" />
Throughout the year, the shrine hosts various other seasonal events. These include the New Year’s Day Festival and Hatsumode on January 1, the Kiuri Tenno-sai and Chigaya-nowa-kuguri festivals in summer, and the Shichi-Go-San festival in autumn. These events hold significant spiritual importance for the local community.<ref name=":0" />

It was established in 1091 by the renowned samurai commander Minamoto No Yoshiie, also known as Hachimantaro. The shrine has a rich history of preserving the Hayashike Bugaku court dance and music, which is recognized as a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. <ref name=":0" />This tradition has been passed down through generations of the Hayashi family priests for over 1,100 years<ref name=":0" />.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Yamagata Pref. Festival & Event｜TOHOKU Cruise Promotion and Cooperation Committee |url=https://www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp/tohoku_cruise/en/020/020/060/ev_yamagata.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp}}</ref> the priests of the shrine come from that family.<ref name=":1" />
== History == The bugaku costumes used in the Hayashike Bugaku performances are distinctively designed with bright crimson cloth, dyed using the benizome method. The surrounding Rokku district was historically inhabited by six families whose members performed alongside the Hayashi family. Descendants of these families continue to be responsible for Hayashike Bugaku performances to this day.<ref name=":0" /> The traditional dance is done on September 14-15 every year.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Benibana Museum (The Safflower Museum) |url=https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/database/benibana/bunken/book/bmuseum/bmuseum3.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp}}</ref>
== festivals == The main festival of the shrine is Donga Matsuri, which is the largest festival in the region. It features a procession with a portable shrine extending up to 2 km in length. Additionally, the festival showcases Yachi Yakko performers and Hayashi Yatai floats with musicians playing festive tunes. An essential part of the Donga Matsuri is the Yayu no bugaku, performed around bonfires at night, providing participants with a solemn atmosphere and a reflection on the eternal flow of time.<ref name=":0" />
Throughout the year, the shrine hosts various other seasonal events. These include the New Year’s Day Festival and Hatsumode on January 1, the Kiuri Tenno-sai and Chigaya-nowa-kuguri festivals in summer, and the Shichi-Go-San festival in autumn. These events hold significant spiritual importance for the local community.<ref name=":0" />

{{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = [[Hachiman]] |image=YamagatakenNishimurayamagun-YachiHachimangu-01.jpg}} The Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine is a historic shrine located in 224 Yachi, Kahoku Town, Nishimurayama County, Yamagata Prefecture<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Japan Heritage Site Yamadera and Safflower Experience » Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine |url=https://yamadera-benibana.jp/experience/en/benibana/%E8%B0%B7%E5%9C%B0%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE/ |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=yamadera-benibana.jp}}</ref>. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]<ref name="bunpu">{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine Names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}}</ref><!--I think this is sufficient citation but it is just about interpreting the name-->
It was established in 1091 by the renowned samurai commander Minamoto No Yoshiie, also known as Hachimantaro. The shrine has a rich history of preserving the Hayashike Bugaku court dance and music, which is recognized as a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. <ref name=":0" />This tradition has been passed down through generations of the Hayashi family priests for over 1,100 years<ref name=":0" />.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Yamagata Pref. Festival & Event｜TOHOKU Cruise Promotion and Cooperation Committee |url=https://www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp/tohoku_cruise/en/020/020/060/ev_yamagata.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp}}</ref> the priests of the shrine come from that family.<ref name=":1" />
== History == The bugaku costumes used in the Hayashike Bugaku performances are distinctively designed with bright crimson cloth, dyed using the benizome method. The surrounding Rokku district was historically inhabited by six families whose members performed alongside the Hayashi family. Descendants of these families continue to be responsible for Hayashike Bugaku performances to this day.<ref name=":0" /> The traditional dance is done on September 14-15 every year.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Benibana Museum (The Safflower Museum) |url=https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/database/benibana/bunken/book/bmuseum/bmuseum3.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp}}</ref>
== festivals == The main festival of the shrine is Donga Matsuri, which is the largest festival in the region. It features a procession with a portable shrine extending up to 2 km in length. Additionally, the festival showcases Yachi Yakko performers and Hayashi Yatai floats with musicians playing festive tunes. An essential part of the Donga Matsuri is the Yayu no bugaku, performed around bonfires at night, providing participants with a solemn atmosphere and a reflection on the eternal flow of time.<ref name=":0" />
Throughout the year, the shrine hosts various other seasonal events. These include the New Year’s Day Festival and Hatsumode on January 1, the Kiuri Tenno-sai and Chigaya-nowa-kuguri festivals in summer, and the Shichi-Go-San festival in autumn. These events hold significant spiritual importance for the local community.<ref name=":0" />

{{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = [[Hachiman]] |image=YamagatakenNishimurayamagun-YachiHachimangu-01.jpg}} The Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine is a historic shrine located in 224 Yachi, Kahoku Town, Nishimurayama County, Yamagata Prefecture<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Japan Heritage Site Yamadera and Safflower Experience » Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine |url=https://yamadera-benibana.jp/experience/en/benibana/%E8%B0%B7%E5%9C%B0%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE/ |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=yamadera-benibana.jp}}</ref>. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]<ref name="bunpu">{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine Names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}}</ref><!--I think this is sufficient citation but it is just about interpreting the name-->
It was established in 1091 by the renowned samurai commander Minamoto No Yoshiie, also known as Hachimantaro. The shrine has a rich history of preserving the Hayashike Bugaku court dance and music, which is recognized as a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. <ref name=":0" />This tradition has been passed down through generations of the Hayashi family priests for over 1,100 years<ref name=":0" />.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Yamagata Pref. Festival & Event｜TOHOKU Cruise Promotion and Cooperation Committee |url=https://www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp/tohoku_cruise/en/020/020/060/ev_yamagata.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp}}</ref> the priests of the shrine come from that family.<ref name=":1" />
== History == The bugaku costumes used in the Hayashike Bugaku performances are distinctively designed with bright crimson cloth, dyed using the benizome method. The surrounding Rokku district was historically inhabited by six families whose members performed alongside the Hayashi family. Descendants of these families continue to be responsible for Hayashike Bugaku performances to this day.<ref name=":0" /> The traditional dance is done on September 14-15 every year.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Benibana Museum (The Safflower Museum) |url=https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/database/benibana/bunken/book/bmuseum/bmuseum3.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp}}</ref>
== festivals == The main festival of the shrine is Donga Matsuri, which is the largest festival in the region. It features a procession with a portable shrine extending up to 2 km in length. Additionally, the festival showcases Yachi Yakko performers and Hayashi Yatai floats with musicians playing festive tunes. An essential part of the Donga Matsuri is the Yayu no bugaku, performed around bonfires at night, providing participants with a solemn atmosphere and a reflection on the eternal flow of time.<ref name=":0" />
Throughout the year, the shrine hosts various other seasonal events. These include the New Year’s Day Festival and Hatsumode on January 1, the Kiuri Tenno-sai and Chigaya-nowa-kuguri festivals in summer, and the Shichi-Go-San festival in autumn. These events hold significant spiritual importance for the local community.<ref name=":0" />

{{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = [[Hachiman]] |image=YamagatakenNishimurayamagun-YachiHachimangu-01.jpg}} The Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine is a historic shrine located in 224 Yachi, Kahoku Town, Nishimurayama County, Yamagata Prefecture<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Japan Heritage Site Yamadera and Safflower Experience » Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine |url=https://yamadera-benibana.jp/experience/en/benibana/%E8%B0%B7%E5%9C%B0%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE/ |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=yamadera-benibana.jp}}</ref>. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]<ref name="bunpu">{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine Names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}}</ref><!--I think this is sufficient citation but it is just about interpreting the name-->
It was established in 1091 by the renowned samurai commander Minamoto No Yoshiie, also known as Hachimantaro. The shrine has a rich history of preserving the Hayashike Bugaku court dance and music, which is recognized as a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. <ref name=":0" />This tradition has been passed down through generations of the Hayashi family priests for over 1,100 years<ref name=":0" />.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Yamagata Pref. Festival & Event｜TOHOKU Cruise Promotion and Cooperation Committee |url=https://www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp/tohoku_cruise/en/020/020/060/ev_yamagata.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp}}</ref> the priests of the shrine come from that family.<ref name=":1" />
== History == The bugaku costumes used in the Hayashike Bugaku performances are distinctively designed with bright crimson cloth, dyed using the benizome method. The surrounding Rokku district was historically inhabited by six families whose members performed alongside the Hayashi family. Descendants of these families continue to be responsible for Hayashike Bugaku performances to this day.<ref name=":0" /> The traditional dance is done on September 14-15 every year.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Benibana Museum (The Safflower Museum) |url=https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/database/benibana/bunken/book/bmuseum/bmuseum3.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp}}</ref>
== festivals == The main festival of the shrine is Donga Matsuri, which is the largest festival in the region. It features a procession with a portable shrine extending up to 2 km in length. Additionally, the festival showcases Yachi Yakko performers and Hayashi Yatai floats with musicians playing festive tunes. An essential part of the Donga Matsuri is the Yayu no bugaku, performed around bonfires at night, providing participants with a solemn atmosphere and a reflection on the eternal flow of time.<ref name=":0" />
Throughout the year, the shrine hosts various other seasonal events. These include the New Year’s Day Festival and Hatsumode on January 1, the Kiuri Tenno-sai and Chigaya-nowa-kuguri festivals in summer, and the Shichi-Go-San festival in autumn. These events hold significant spiritual importance for the local community.<ref name=":0" />

It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]<ref name="bunpu">{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine Names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}}</ref><!--I think this is sufficient citation but it is just about interpreting the name-->
It was established in 1091 by the renowned samurai commander Minamoto No Yoshiie, also known as Hachimantaro. The shrine has a rich history of preserving the Hayashike Bugaku court dance and music, which is recognized as a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. <ref name=":0" />This tradition has been passed down through generations of the Hayashi family priests for over 1,100 years<ref name=":0" />.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Yamagata Pref. Festival & Event｜TOHOKU Cruise Promotion and Cooperation Committee |url=https://www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp/tohoku_cruise/en/020/020/060/ev_yamagata.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp}}</ref> the priests of the shrine come from that family.<ref name=":1" />
== History == The bugaku costumes used in the Hayashike Bugaku performances are distinctively designed with bright crimson cloth, dyed using the benizome method. The surrounding Rokku district was historically inhabited by six families whose members performed alongside the Hayashi family. Descendants of these families continue to be responsible for Hayashike Bugaku performances to this day.<ref name=":0" /> The traditional dance is done on September 14-15 every year.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Benibana Museum (The Safflower Museum) |url=https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/database/benibana/bunken/book/bmuseum/bmuseum3.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp}}</ref>
== festivals == The main festival of the shrine is Donga Matsuri, which is the largest festival in the region. It features a procession with a portable shrine extending up to 2 km in length. Additionally, the festival showcases Yachi Yakko performers and Hayashi Yatai floats with musicians playing festive tunes. An essential part of the Donga Matsuri is the Yayu no bugaku, performed around bonfires at night, providing participants with a solemn atmosphere and a reflection on the eternal flow of time.<ref name=":0" />
Throughout the year, the shrine hosts various other seasonal events. These include the New Year’s Day Festival and Hatsumode on January 1, the Kiuri Tenno-sai and Chigaya-nowa-kuguri festivals in summer, and the Shichi-Go-San festival in autumn. These events hold significant spiritual importance for the local community.<ref name=":0" />

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original at [[every:Yachi Hachimangu]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 12 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = [[Hachiman]] |image=YamagatakenNishimurayamagun-YachiHachimangu-01.jpg}} The Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine is a historic shrine located in 224 Yachi, Kahoku Town, Nishimurayama County, Yamagata Prefecture<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Japan Heritage Site Yamadera and Safflower Experience » Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine |url=https://yamadera-benibana.jp/experience/en/benibana/%E8%B0%B7%E5%9C%B0%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE/ |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=yamadera-benibana.jp}}</ref>. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]<ref name="bunpu">{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine Names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}}</ref><!--I think this is sufficient citation but it is just about interpreting the name-->
It was established in 1091 by the renowned samurai commander Minamoto No Yoshiie, also known as Hachimantaro. The shrine has a rich history of preserving the Hayashike Bugaku court dance and music, which is recognized as a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. <ref name=":0" />This tradition has been passed down through generations of the Hayashi family priests for over 1,100 years<ref name=":0" />.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Yamagata Pref. Festival & Event｜TOHOKU Cruise Promotion and Cooperation Committee |url=https://www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp/tohoku_cruise/en/020/020/060/ev_yamagata.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp}}</ref> the priests of the shrine come from that family.<ref name=":1" />
== History == The bugaku costumes used in the Hayashike Bugaku performances are distinctively designed with bright crimson cloth, dyed using the benizome method. The surrounding Rokku district was historically inhabited by six families whose members performed alongside the Hayashi family. Descendants of these families continue to be responsible for Hayashike Bugaku performances to this day.<ref name=":0" /> The traditional dance is done on September 14-15 every year.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Benibana Museum (The Safflower Museum) |url=https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/database/benibana/bunken/book/bmuseum/bmuseum3.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp}}</ref>
== festivals == The main festival of the shrine is Donga Matsuri, which is the largest festival in the region. It features a procession with a portable shrine extending up to 2 km in length. Additionally, the festival showcases Yachi Yakko performers and Hayashi Yatai floats with musicians playing festive tunes. An essential part of the Donga Matsuri is the Yayu no bugaku, performed around bonfires at night, providing participants with a solemn atmosphere and a reflection on the eternal flow of time.<ref name=":0" />
Throughout the year, the shrine hosts various other seasonal events. These include the New Year’s Day Festival and Hatsumode on January 1, the Kiuri Tenno-sai and Chigaya-nowa-kuguri festivals in summer, and the Shichi-Go-San festival in autumn. These events hold significant spiritual importance for the local community.<ref name=":0" />

Categorizing it as such was an error. It is from an archived english wikipedia draft-->}} [[ja:%E8%B0%B7%E5%9C%B0%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yachi Hachimangū]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:achi Hachimangū]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yachi Hachimangū]][[Category:11]] \[[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yachi Hachimangū]][[Category:11]] [[Category:tier 5]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category: 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yachi Hachimangū]][[Category:11]] [[Category:tier 5]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category: 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yachi Hachimangū]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yachi Hachimangū]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yachi Hachimangū]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yachi Hachimangū]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yachi Hachimangū]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yachi Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yachi Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = [[Hachiman]] |image=YamagatakenNishimurayamagun-YachiHachimangu-01.jpg}} The Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine is a historic shrine located in 224 Yachi, Kahoku Town, Nishimurayama County, Yamagata Prefecture<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Japan Heritage Site Yamadera and Safflower Experience » Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine |url=https://yamadera-benibana.jp/experience/en/benibana/%E8%B0%B7%E5%9C%B0%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE/ |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=yamadera-benibana.jp}}</ref>. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]<ref name="bunpu">{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine Names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}}</ref><!--I think this is sufficient citation but it is just about interpreting the name-->
It was established in 1091 by the renowned samurai commander Minamoto No Yoshiie, also known as Hachimantaro. The shrine has a rich history of preserving the Hayashike Bugaku court dance and music, which is recognized as a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. <ref name=":0" />This tradition has been passed down through generations of the Hayashi family priests for over 1,100 years<ref name=":0" />.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Yamagata Pref. Festival & Event｜TOHOKU Cruise Promotion and Cooperation Committee |url=https://www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp/tohoku_cruise/en/020/020/060/ev_yamagata.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp}}</ref> the priests of the shrine come from that family.<ref name=":1" />
== History == The bugaku costumes used in the Hayashike Bugaku performances are distinctively designed with bright crimson cloth, dyed using the benizome method. The surrounding Rokku district was historically inhabited by six families whose members performed alongside the Hayashi family. Descendants of these families continue to be responsible for Hayashike Bugaku performances to this day.<ref name=":0" /> The traditional dance is done on September 14-15 every year.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Benibana Museum (The Safflower Museum) |url=https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/database/benibana/bunken/book/bmuseum/bmuseum3.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp}}</ref>
== festivals == The main festival of the shrine is Donga Matsuri, which is the largest festival in the region. It features a procession with a portable shrine extending up to 2 km in length. Additionally, the festival showcases Yachi Yakko performers and Hayashi Yatai floats with musicians playing festive tunes. An essential part of the Donga Matsuri is the Yayu no bugaku, performed around bonfires at night, providing participants with a solemn atmosphere and a reflection on the eternal flow of time.<ref name=":0" />
Throughout the year, the shrine hosts various other seasonal events. These include the New Year’s Day Festival and Hatsumode on January 1, the Kiuri Tenno-sai and Chigaya-nowa-kuguri festivals in summer, and the Shichi-Go-San festival in autumn. These events hold significant spiritual importance for the local community.<ref name=":0" />

{{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = [[Hachiman]] |image=YamagatakenNishimurayamagun-YachiHachimangu-01.jpg}} The Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine is a historic shrine located in 224 Yachi, Kahoku Town, Nishimurayama County, Yamagata Prefecture<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Japan Heritage Site Yamadera and Safflower Experience » Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine |url=https://yamadera-benibana.jp/experience/en/benibana/%E8%B0%B7%E5%9C%B0%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE/ |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=yamadera-benibana.jp}}</ref>. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]<ref name="bunpu">{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine Names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}}</ref><!--I think this is sufficient citation but it is just about interpreting the name-->
It was established in 1091 by the renowned samurai commander Minamoto No Yoshiie, also known as Hachimantaro. The shrine has a rich history of preserving the Hayashike Bugaku court dance and music, which is recognized as a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. <ref name=":0" />This tradition has been passed down through generations of the Hayashi family priests for over 1,100 years<ref name=":0" />.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Yamagata Pref. Festival & Event｜TOHOKU Cruise Promotion and Cooperation Committee |url=https://www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp/tohoku_cruise/en/020/020/060/ev_yamagata.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp}}</ref> the priests of the shrine come from that family.<ref name=":1" />
== History == The bugaku costumes used in the Hayashike Bugaku performances are distinctively designed with bright crimson cloth, dyed using the benizome method. The surrounding Rokku district was historically inhabited by six families whose members performed alongside the Hayashi family. Descendants of these families continue to be responsible for Hayashike Bugaku performances to this day.<ref name=":0" /> The traditional dance is done on September 14-15 every year.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Benibana Museum (The Safflower Museum) |url=https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/database/benibana/bunken/book/bmuseum/bmuseum3.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp}}</ref>
== festivals == The main festival of the shrine is Donga Matsuri, which is the largest festival in the region. It features a procession with a portable shrine extending up to 2 km in length. Additionally, the festival showcases Yachi Yakko performers and Hayashi Yatai floats with musicians playing festive tunes. An essential part of the Donga Matsuri is the Yayu no bugaku, performed around bonfires at night, providing participants with a solemn atmosphere and a reflection on the eternal flow of time.<ref name=":0" />
Throughout the year, the shrine hosts various other seasonal events. These include the New Year’s Day Festival and Hatsumode on January 1, the Kiuri Tenno-sai and Chigaya-nowa-kuguri festivals in summer, and the Shichi-Go-San festival in autumn. These events hold significant spiritual importance for the local community.<ref name=":0" />

{{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = [[Hachiman]] |image=YamagatakenNishimurayamagun-YachiHachimangu-01.jpg}} The Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine is a historic shrine located in 224 Yachi, Kahoku Town, Nishimurayama County, Yamagata Prefecture<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Japan Heritage Site Yamadera and Safflower Experience » Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine |url=https://yamadera-benibana.jp/experience/en/benibana/%E8%B0%B7%E5%9C%B0%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE/ |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=yamadera-benibana.jp}}</ref>. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]<ref name="bunpu">{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine Names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}}</ref><!--I think this is sufficient citation but it is just about interpreting the name-->
It was established in 1091 by the renowned samurai commander Minamoto No Yoshiie, also known as Hachimantaro. The shrine has a rich history of preserving the Hayashike Bugaku court dance and music, which is recognized as a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. <ref name=":0" />This tradition has been passed down through generations of the Hayashi family priests for over 1,100 years<ref name=":0" />.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Yamagata Pref. Festival & Event｜TOHOKU Cruise Promotion and Cooperation Committee |url=https://www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp/tohoku_cruise/en/020/020/060/ev_yamagata.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp}}</ref> the priests of the shrine come from that family.<ref name=":1" />
== History == The bugaku costumes used in the Hayashike Bugaku performances are distinctively designed with bright crimson cloth, dyed using the benizome method. The surrounding Rokku district was historically inhabited by six families whose members performed alongside the Hayashi family. Descendants of these families continue to be responsible for Hayashike Bugaku performances to this day.<ref name=":0" /> The traditional dance is done on September 14-15 every year.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Benibana Museum (The Safflower Museum) |url=https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/database/benibana/bunken/book/bmuseum/bmuseum3.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp}}</ref>
== festivals == The main festival of the shrine is Donga Matsuri, which is the largest festival in the region. It features a procession with a portable shrine extending up to 2 km in length. Additionally, the festival showcases Yachi Yakko performers and Hayashi Yatai floats with musicians playing festive tunes. An essential part of the Donga Matsuri is the Yayu no bugaku, performed around bonfires at night, providing participants with a solemn atmosphere and a reflection on the eternal flow of time.<ref name=":0" />
Throughout the year, the shrine hosts various other seasonal events. These include the New Year’s Day Festival and Hatsumode on January 1, the Kiuri Tenno-sai and Chigaya-nowa-kuguri festivals in summer, and the Shichi-Go-San festival in autumn. These events hold significant spiritual importance for the local community.<ref name=":0" />

== festivals == The main festival of the shrine is Donga Matsuri, which is the largest festival in the region. It features a procession with a portable shrine extending up to 2 km in length. Additionally, the festival showcases Yachi Yakko performers and Hayashi Yatai floats with musicians playing festive tunes. An essential part of the Donga Matsuri is the Yayu no bugaku, performed around bonfires at night, providing participants with a solemn atmosphere and a reflection on the eternal flow of time.<ref name=":0" />
Throughout the year, the shrine hosts various other seasonal events. These include the New Year’s Day Festival and Hatsumode on January 1, the Kiuri Tenno-sai and Chigaya-nowa-kuguri festivals in summer, and the Shichi-Go-San festival in autumn. These events hold significant spiritual importance for the local community.<ref name=":0" />

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original at [[every:Yachi Hachimangu]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 12 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = [[Hachiman]] |image=YamagatakenNishimurayamagun-YachiHachimangu-01.jpg}} The Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine is a historic shrine located in 224 Yachi, Kahoku Town, Nishimurayama County, Yamagata Prefecture<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Japan Heritage Site Yamadera and Safflower Experience » Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine |url=https://yamadera-benibana.jp/experience/en/benibana/%E8%B0%B7%E5%9C%B0%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE/ |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=yamadera-benibana.jp}}</ref>. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]<ref name="bunpu">{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine Names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}}</ref><!--I think this is sufficient citation but it is just about interpreting the name-->
It was established in 1091 by the renowned samurai commander Minamoto No Yoshiie, also known as Hachimantaro. The shrine has a rich history of preserving the Hayashike Bugaku court dance and music, which is recognized as a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. <ref name=":0" />This tradition has been passed down through generations of the Hayashi family priests for over 1,100 years<ref name=":0" />.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Yamagata Pref. Festival & Event｜TOHOKU Cruise Promotion and Cooperation Committee |url=https://www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp/tohoku_cruise/en/020/020/060/ev_yamagata.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp}}</ref> the priests of the shrine come from that family.<ref name=":1" />
== History == The bugaku costumes used in the Hayashike Bugaku performances are distinctively designed with bright crimson cloth, dyed using the benizome method. The surrounding Rokku district was historically inhabited by six families whose members performed alongside the Hayashi family. Descendants of these families continue to be responsible for Hayashike Bugaku performances to this day.<ref name=":0" /> The traditional dance is done on September 14-15 every year.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Benibana Museum (The Safflower Museum) |url=https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/database/benibana/bunken/book/bmuseum/bmuseum3.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp}}</ref>
== festivals == The main festival of the shrine is Donga Matsuri, which is the largest festival in the region. It features a procession with a portable shrine extending up to 2 km in length. Additionally, the festival showcases Yachi Yakko performers and Hayashi Yatai floats with musicians playing festive tunes. An essential part of the Donga Matsuri is the Yayu no bugaku, performed around bonfires at night, providing participants with a solemn atmosphere and a reflection on the eternal flow of time.<ref name=":0" />
Throughout the year, the shrine hosts various other seasonal events. These include the New Year’s Day Festival and Hatsumode on January 1, the Kiuri Tenno-sai and Chigaya-nowa-kuguri festivals in summer, and the Shichi-Go-San festival in autumn. These events hold significant spiritual importance for the local community.<ref name=":0" />

{{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = [[Hachiman]] |image=YamagatakenNishimurayamagun-YachiHachimangu-01.jpg}} The Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine is a historic shrine located in 224 Yachi, Kahoku Town, Nishimurayama County, Yamagata Prefecture<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Japan Heritage Site Yamadera and Safflower Experience » Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine |url=https://yamadera-benibana.jp/experience/en/benibana/%E8%B0%B7%E5%9C%B0%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE/ |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=yamadera-benibana.jp}}</ref>. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]<ref name="bunpu">{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine Names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}}</ref><!--I think this is sufficient citation but it is just about interpreting the name-->
It was established in 1091 by the renowned samurai commander Minamoto No Yoshiie, also known as Hachimantaro. The shrine has a rich history of preserving the Hayashike Bugaku court dance and music, which is recognized as a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. <ref name=":0" />This tradition has been passed down through generations of the Hayashi family priests for over 1,100 years<ref name=":0" />.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Yamagata Pref. Festival & Event｜TOHOKU Cruise Promotion and Cooperation Committee |url=https://www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp/tohoku_cruise/en/020/020/060/ev_yamagata.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp}}</ref> the priests of the shrine come from that family.<ref name=":1" />
== History == The bugaku costumes used in the Hayashike Bugaku performances are distinctively designed with bright crimson cloth, dyed using the benizome method. The surrounding Rokku district was historically inhabited by six families whose members performed alongside the Hayashi family. Descendants of these families continue to be responsible for Hayashike Bugaku performances to this day.<ref name=":0" /> The traditional dance is done on September 14-15 every year.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Benibana Museum (The Safflower Museum) |url=https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/database/benibana/bunken/book/bmuseum/bmuseum3.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp}}</ref>
== festivals == The main festival of the shrine is Donga Matsuri, which is the largest festival in the region. It features a procession with a portable shrine extending up to 2 km in length. Additionally, the festival showcases Yachi Yakko performers and Hayashi Yatai floats with musicians playing festive tunes. An essential part of the Donga Matsuri is the Yayu no bugaku, performed around bonfires at night, providing participants with a solemn atmosphere and a reflection on the eternal flow of time.<ref name=":0" />
Throughout the year, the shrine hosts various other seasonal events. These include the New Year’s Day Festival and Hatsumode on January 1, the Kiuri Tenno-sai and Chigaya-nowa-kuguri festivals in summer, and the Shichi-Go-San festival in autumn. These events hold significant spiritual importance for the local community.<ref name=":0" />

{{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yachi Hachimangū]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:achi Hachimangū]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yachi Hachimangū]][[Category:11]] \[[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yachi Hachimangū]][[Category:11]] [[Category: 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yachi Hachimangū]][[Category:11]] [[Category: 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yachi Hachimangū]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yachi Hachimangū]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yachi Hachimangū]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yachi Hachimangū]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yachi Hachimangū]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yachi Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yachi Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} [[Category:completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yachi Hachimangū]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original at [[every:Yachi Hachimangu]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 12 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = [[Hachiman]] |image=YamagatakenNishimurayamagun-YachiHachimangu-01.jpg}} The Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine is a historic shrine located in 224 Yachi, Kahoku Town, Nishimurayama County, Yamagata Prefecture<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Japan Heritage Site Yamadera and Safflower Experience » Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine |url=https://yamadera-benibana.jp/experience/en/benibana/%E8%B0%B7%E5%9C%B0%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE/ |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=yamadera-benibana.jp}}</ref>. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]<ref name="bunpu">{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine Names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}}</ref><!--I think this is sufficient citation but it is just about interpreting the name-->
It was established in 1091 by the renowned samurai commander Minamoto No Yoshiie, also known as Hachimantaro. The shrine has a rich history of preserving the Hayashike Bugaku court dance and music, which is recognized as a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. <ref name=":0" />This tradition has been passed down through generations of the Hayashi family priests for over 1,100 years<ref name=":0" />.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Yamagata Pref. Festival & Event｜TOHOKU Cruise Promotion and Cooperation Committee |url=https://www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp/tohoku_cruise/en/020/020/060/ev_yamagata.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp}}</ref> the priests of the shrine come from that family.<ref name=":1" />
== History == The bugaku costumes used in the Hayashike Bugaku performances are distinctively designed with bright crimson cloth, dyed using the benizome method. The surrounding Rokku district was historically inhabited by six families whose members performed alongside the Hayashi family. Descendants of these families continue to be responsible for Hayashike Bugaku performances to this day.<ref name=":0" /> The traditional dance is done on September 14-15 every year.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Benibana Museum (The Safflower Museum) |url=https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/database/benibana/bunken/book/bmuseum/bmuseum3.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp}}</ref>
== festivals == The main festival of the shrine is Donga Matsuri, which is the largest festival in the region. It features a procession with a portable shrine extending up to 2 km in length. Additionally, the festival showcases Yachi Yakko performers and Hayashi Yatai floats with musicians playing festive tunes. An essential part of the Donga Matsuri is the Yayu no bugaku, performed around bonfires at night, providing participants with a solemn atmosphere and a reflection on the eternal flow of time.<ref name=":0" />
Throughout the year, the shrine hosts various other seasonal events. These include the New Year’s Day Festival and Hatsumode on January 1, the Kiuri Tenno-sai and Chigaya-nowa-kuguri festivals in summer, and the Shichi-Go-San festival in autumn. These events hold significant spiritual importance for the local community.<ref name=":0" />

{{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = [[Hachiman]] |image=YamagatakenNishimurayamagun-YachiHachimangu-01.jpg}} The Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine is a historic shrine located in 224 Yachi, Kahoku Town, Nishimurayama County, Yamagata Prefecture<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Japan Heritage Site Yamadera and Safflower Experience » Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine |url=https://yamadera-benibana.jp/experience/en/benibana/%E8%B0%B7%E5%9C%B0%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE/ |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=yamadera-benibana.jp}}</ref>. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]<ref name="bunpu">{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine Names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}}</ref><!--I think this is sufficient citation but it is just about interpreting the name-->
It was established in 1091 by the renowned samurai commander Minamoto No Yoshiie, also known as Hachimantaro. The shrine has a rich history of preserving the Hayashike Bugaku court dance and music, which is recognized as a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. <ref name=":0" />This tradition has been passed down through generations of the Hayashi family priests for over 1,100 years<ref name=":0" />.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Yamagata Pref. Festival & Event｜TOHOKU Cruise Promotion and Cooperation Committee |url=https://www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp/tohoku_cruise/en/020/020/060/ev_yamagata.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp}}</ref> the priests of the shrine come from that family.<ref name=":1" />
== History == The bugaku costumes used in the Hayashike Bugaku performances are distinctively designed with bright crimson cloth, dyed using the benizome method. The surrounding Rokku district was historically inhabited by six families whose members performed alongside the Hayashi family. Descendants of these families continue to be responsible for Hayashike Bugaku performances to this day.<ref name=":0" /> The traditional dance is done on September 14-15 every year.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Benibana Museum (The Safflower Museum) |url=https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/database/benibana/bunken/book/bmuseum/bmuseum3.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp}}</ref>

These events hold significant spiritual importance for the local community.<ref name=":0" />

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2020年3月}} {{神社 | 名称=谷地八幡宮 | 画像=[[File:YamagatakenNishimurayamagun-YachiHachimangu-01.jpg|260px|谷地八幡宮・拝殿]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[山形県]][[西村山郡]][[河北町]]谷地乙224 | 位置={{Coord|38|25|33.7|N|140|18|47|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=谷地八幡宮}} | 祭神=[[応神天皇]] | 創建=[[寛治]]5年（[[1091年]]） | 社格=旧[[県社]]
[[別表神社]] | 例祭=[[9月14日]]・[[9月15日|15日]] | 神事=林家舞楽|地図={{Location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=25|lat_sec=33.77|lon_deg=140|lon_min=18|lon_sec=47.01|label=谷地
八幡宮|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|25|33.7|N|140|18|47|E}}}} }} '''谷地八幡宮'''（やちはちまんぐう）は、[[山形県]][[西村山郡]][[河北町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 [[応神天皇]]（[[八幡神]]）を主[[祭神]]とする。 == 歴史 == [[寛治]]5年（[[1091年]]）、[[鎮守府将軍]]・[[源義家]]が、神恩に感謝して[[石清水八幡宮|石清水]]から白鳥村（現 [[村山市]]白鳥近辺）に八幡神を[[勧請]]して祭祀を行ったのに始まると伝えられる。[[天正]]年間（[[1573年]] - [[1592年]]）頃、[[谷地城]]主・[[白鳥長久]]が白鳥村から現在地に遷し、鎮守とした。[[寒河江八幡宮]]・[[溝延八幡宮]]とともに「[[寒河江荘]]三八幡」と称され、武家の崇敬を受けた。 かつては八幡神社と称していたが、神社本庁の別表神社に加列される際に谷地八幡宮に改称した。 == 祭事 == 9月の[[例祭]]で奉納される「谷地の舞楽」は、[[大阪]]・[[四天王寺]]の[[三方楽所#楽家|楽人]]の流れをくむ[[神職]]・林家が一子相伝で[[伝承]]しているもので、「[[林家舞楽]]」とも呼ばれ[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている。林家舞楽は[[宮内庁式部職楽部|宮中舞楽]]・[[聖霊会の舞楽|四天王寺舞楽]]・[[三方楽所|南都楽所]][[舞楽]]と並ぶ日本四大舞楽の一つとされる。 ; 歳旦祭 : 1月1日。午前零時に一番太鼓が打たれ、初詣が始る。神殿では、歳旦祭の祝詞が朗々と奏上され、神前に舞楽が奉奏される。 ; おさいとう : 1月14日。17：30に点火し、正月に飾った注連飾りや古神札、古お守りを炊き上げる。 ; 産業祭 : 1月中旬。社業の繁栄・操業の安全を祈願する。 ; 紀元祭 : 2月11日。皇室国家の弥栄、氏子崇敬者の平安を祈願する。 ; 祈年祭 : 3月下旬。皇室の弥栄と国の隆昌、氏子崇敬者の家内安全、そして当地方の五穀豊穣、産業振興を祈願する。 ; 昭和祭 : 4月29日。[[昭和天皇]]の聖徳大業を景仰し、皇室の弥栄と国家国民の繁栄を祈願する。 ; 夏越の大祓 : 7月海の日。茅の輪神事を行い厄を払う。 ; きゅうり天王祭 : 7月海の日。境内末社[[八坂神社]]の例祭。きゅうりを供え、厄病退散を祈願する。 ; 風日祈祭(かざひのみさい)・宮参り : 八月最終土曜日。秋の収穫まで風雨の災いがないように祈る風祭り、五穀の豊穣を祈る宮参り。 ; 二百十日祭・神幸の儀 : 八月最終日曜日。五穀豊穣・国家繁栄・家内安全の祈願のため、神輿が谷地地区内を渡御する。 ; 例大祭 : 2014年9月12日　20：00～　楽揃式・奴祓式、9月13日　例大祭1日目　13：30～　例祭祓式　、14：20～　舞楽奉奏（7曲）など、9月14日　例大祭2日目　12：00～　神幸所発輿祭　、14：00～　舞楽奉奏（10曲）など、9月15日　例大祭3日目　13：30～　大黒舞など。 ; 神嘗祭奉祝祭 : 10月20日。[[伊勢神宮]]の神嘗祭に合わせて行われる。 ; 明治祭 : 11月3日。[[明治天皇]]の聖徳大業を景仰し、皇室の弥栄と国家国民の繁栄を祈願する。 ; 新嘗祭・神宮大麻頒布始祭 : 11月23日。神前に新穀を供え、五穀豊穣を感謝する。 ; 天長祭 : 12月23日（天皇御誕生日）。天皇の誕生を氏子崇敬者と共に祝い、皇室の弥栄を祈念する。 ; 師走の大祓 : 12月31日。新年を迎えるに当たり厄を払う。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.yachihachimangu.jp/ 谷地八幡宮HP] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やちはちまんくう}} [[Category:山形県の神社]] [[Category:山形県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:河北町の建築物]] [[Category:河北町の歴史]]

{{出典の明記|date=2020年3月}} {{神社 | 名称=谷地八幡宮 | 画像=[[File:YamagatakenNishimurayamagun-YachiHachimangu-01.jpg|260px|谷地八幡宮・拝殿]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[山形県]][[西村山郡]][[河北町]]谷地乙224 | 位置={{Coord|38|25|33.7|N|140|18|47|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=谷地八幡宮}} | 祭神=[[応神天皇]] | 創建=[[寛治]]5年（[[1091年]]） | 社格=旧[[県社]]
[[別表神社]] | 例祭=[[9月14日]]・[[9月15日|15日]] | 神事=林家舞楽|地図={{Location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=25|lat_sec=33.77|lon_deg=140|lon_min=18|lon_sec=47.01|label=谷地
八幡宮|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|25|33.7|N|140|18|47|E}}}} }} '''谷地八幡宮'''（やちはちまんぐう）は、[[山形県]][[西村山郡]][[河北町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 [[応神天皇]]（[[八幡神]]）を主[[祭神]]とする。 == 歴史 == [[寛治]]5年（[[1091年]]）、[[鎮守府将軍]]・[[源義家]]が、神恩に感謝して[[石清水八幡宮|石清水]]から白鳥村（現 [[村山市]]白鳥近辺）に八幡神を[[勧請]]して祭祀を行ったのに始まると伝えられる。[[天正]]年間（[[1573年]] - [[1592年]]）頃、[[谷地城]]主・[[白鳥長久]]が白鳥村から現在地に遷し、鎮守とした。[[寒河江八幡宮]]・[[溝延八幡宮]]とともに「[[寒河江荘]]三八幡」と称され、武家の崇敬を受けた。 かつては八幡神社と称していたが、神社本庁の別表神社に加列される際に谷地八幡宮に改称した。 == 祭事 == 9月の[[例祭]]で奉納される「谷地の舞楽」は、[[大阪]]・[[四天王寺]]の[[三方楽所#楽家|楽人]]の流れをくむ[[神職]]・林家が一子相伝で[[伝承]]しているもので、「[[林家舞楽]]」とも呼ばれ[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている。林家舞楽は[[宮内庁式部職楽部|宮中舞楽]]・[[聖霊会の舞楽|四天王寺舞楽]]・[[三方楽所|南都楽所]][[舞楽]]と並ぶ日本四大舞楽の一つとされる。 ; 歳旦祭 : 1月1日。午前零時に一番太鼓が打たれ、初詣が始る。神殿では、歳旦祭の祝詞が朗々と奏上され、神前に舞楽が奉奏される。 ; おさいとう : 1月14日。17：30に点火し、正月に飾った注連飾りや古神札、古お守りを炊き上げる。 ; 産業祭 : 1月中旬。社業の繁栄・操業の安全を祈願する。 ; 紀元祭 : 2月11日。皇室国家の弥栄、氏子崇敬者の平安を祈願する。 ; 祈年祭 : 3月下旬。皇室の弥栄と国の隆昌、氏子崇敬者の家内安全、そして当地方の五穀豊穣、産業振興を祈願する。 ; 昭和祭 : 4月29日。[[昭和天皇]]の聖徳大業を景仰し、皇室の弥栄と国家国民の繁栄を祈願する。 ; 夏越の大祓 : 7月海の日。茅の輪神事を行い厄を払う。 ; きゅうり天王祭 : 7月海の日。境内末社[[八坂神社]]の例祭。きゅうりを供え、厄病退散を祈願する。 ; 風日祈祭(かざひのみさい)・宮参り : 八月最終土曜日。秋の収穫まで風雨の災いがないように祈る風祭り、五穀の豊穣を祈る宮参り。 ; 二百十日祭・神幸の儀 : 八月最終日曜日。五穀豊穣・国家繁栄・家内安全の祈願のため、神輿が谷地地区内を渡御する。 ; 例大祭 : 2014年9月12日　20：00～　楽揃式・奴祓式、9月13日　例大祭1日目　13：30～　例祭祓式　、14：20～　舞楽奉奏（7曲）など、9月14日　例大祭2日目　12：00～　神幸所発輿祭　、14：00～　舞楽奉奏（10曲）など、9月15日　例大祭3日目　13：30～　大黒舞など。 ; 神嘗祭奉祝祭 : 10月20日。[[伊勢神宮]]の神嘗祭に合わせて行われる。 ; 明治祭 : 11月3日。[[明治天皇]]の聖徳大業を景仰し、皇室の弥栄と国家国民の繁栄を祈願する。 ; 新嘗祭・神宮大麻頒布始祭 : 11月23日。神前に新穀を供え、五穀豊穣を感謝する。 ; 天長祭 : 12月23日（天皇御誕生日）。天皇の誕生を氏子崇敬者と共に祝い、皇室の弥栄を祈念する。 ; 師走の大祓 : 12月31日。新年を迎えるに当たり厄を払う。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.yachihachimangu.jp/ 谷地八幡宮HP] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やちはちまんくう}} [[Category:山形県の神社]] [[Category:山形県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:河北町の建築物]] [[Category:河北町の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original at [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 12 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = [[Hachiman]] |image=YamagatakenNishimurayamagun-YachiHachimangu-01.jpg}} The Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine is a historic shrine located in 224 Yachi, Kahoku Town, Nishimurayama County, Yamagata Prefecture<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Japan Heritage Site Yamadera and Safflower Experience » Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine |url=https://yamadera-benibana.jp/experience/en/benibana/%E8%B0%B7%E5%9C%B0%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE/ |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=yamadera-benibana.jp}}</ref>. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]<ref name="bunpu">{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine Names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}}</ref>
It was established in 1091 by the renowned samurai commander Minamoto No Yoshiie, also known as Hachimantaro. The shrine has a rich history of preserving the Hayashike Bugaku court dance and music, which is recognized as a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. <ref name=":0" />This tradition has been passed down through generations of the Hayashi family priests for over 1,100 years<ref name=":0" />.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Yamagata Pref. Festival & Event｜TOHOKU Cruise Promotion and Cooperation Committee |url=https://www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp/tohoku_cruise/en/020/020/060/ev_yamagata.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp}}</ref> the priests of the shrine come from that family.<ref name=":1" />
== History == The bugaku costumes used in the Hayashike Bugaku performances are distinctively designed with bright crimson cloth, dyed using the benizome method. The surrounding Rokku district was historically inhabited by six families whose members performed alongside the Hayashi family. Descendants of these families continue to be responsible for Hayashike Bugaku performances to this day.<ref name=":0" /> The traditional dance is done on September 14-15 every year.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Benibana Museum (The Safflower Museum) |url=https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/database/benibana/bunken/book/bmuseum/bmuseum3.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp}}</ref>
== festivals == The main festival of the shrine is Donga Matsuri, which is the largest festival in the region. It features a procession with a portable shrine extending up to 2 km in length. Additionally, the festival showcases Yachi Yakko performers and Hayashi Yatai floats with musicians playing festive tunes. An essential part of the Donga Matsuri is the Yayu no bugaku, performed around bonfires at night, providing participants with a solemn atmosphere and a reflection on the eternal flow of time.<ref name=":0" />
Throughout the year, the shrine hosts various other seasonal events. These include the New Year’s Day Festival and Hatsumode on January 1, the Kiuri Tenno-sai and Chigaya-nowa-kuguri festivals in summer, and the Shichi-Go-San festival in autumn. These events hold significant spiritual importance for the local community.<ref name=":0" />
== References == {{reflist}}

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:河北町の建築物]] into [[Category:Buildings in Kahoku Town]] via bot
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original at [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 12 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = [[Hachiman]] |image=YamagatakenNishimurayamagun-YachiHachimangu-01.jpg}} The Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine is a historic shrine located in 224 Yachi, Kahoku Town, Nishimurayama County, Yamagata Prefecture<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Japan Heritage Site Yamadera and Safflower Experience » Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine |url=https://yamadera-benibana.jp/experience/en/benibana/%E8%B0%B7%E5%9C%B0%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE/ |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=yamadera-benibana.jp}}</ref>. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]<ref name="bunpu">{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine Names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}}</ref>
It was established in 1091 by the renowned samurai commander Minamoto No Yoshiie, also known as Hachimantaro. The shrine has a rich history of preserving the Hayashike Bugaku court dance and music, which is recognized as a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. <ref name=":0" />This tradition has been passed down through generations of the Hayashi family priests for over 1,100 years<ref name=":0" />.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Yamagata Pref. Festival & Event｜TOHOKU Cruise Promotion and Cooperation Committee |url=https://www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp/tohoku_cruise/en/020/020/060/ev_yamagata.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp}}</ref> the priests of the shrine come from that family.<ref name=":1" />
== History == The bugaku costumes used in the Hayashike Bugaku performances are distinctively designed with bright crimson cloth, dyed using the benizome method. The surrounding Rokku district was historically inhabited by six families whose members performed alongside the Hayashi family. Descendants of these families continue to be responsible for Hayashike Bugaku performances to this day.<ref name=":0" /> The traditional dance is done on September 14-15 every year.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Benibana Museum (The Safflower Museum) |url=https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/database/benibana/bunken/book/bmuseum/bmuseum3.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp}}</ref>
== festivals == The main festival of the shrine is Donga Matsuri, which is the largest festival in the region. It features a procession with a portable shrine extending up to 2 km in length. Additionally, the festival showcases Yachi Yakko performers and Hayashi Yatai floats with musicians playing festive tunes. An essential part of the Donga Matsuri is the Yayu no bugaku, performed around bonfires at night, providing participants with a solemn atmosphere and a reflection on the eternal flow of time.<ref name=":0" />
Throughout the year, the shrine hosts various other seasonal events. These include the New Year’s Day Festival and Hatsumode on January 1, the Kiuri Tenno-sai and Chigaya-nowa-kuguri festivals in summer, and the Shichi-Go-San festival in autumn. These events hold significant spiritual importance for the local community.<ref name=":0" />
== References == {{reflist}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11632782]] Wikidata name: Yachi-hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original at [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 12 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = [[Hachiman]] |image=YamagatakenNishimurayamagun-YachiHachimangu-01.jpg}} The Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine is a historic shrine located in 224 Yachi, Kahoku Town, Nishimurayama County, Yamagata Prefecture<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Japan Heritage Site Yamadera and Safflower Experience » Yachi Hachiman-gu Shrine |url=https://yamadera-benibana.jp/experience/en/benibana/%E8%B0%B7%E5%9C%B0%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE/ |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=yamadera-benibana.jp}}</ref>. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]<ref name="bunpu">{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine Names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}}</ref>
It was established in 1091 by the renowned samurai commander Minamoto No Yoshiie, also known as Hachimantaro. The shrine has a rich history of preserving the Hayashike Bugaku court dance and music, which is recognized as a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. <ref name=":0" />This tradition has been passed down through generations of the Hayashi family priests for over 1,100 years<ref name=":0" />.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Yamagata Pref. Festival & Event｜TOHOKU Cruise Promotion and Cooperation Committee |url=https://www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp/tohoku_cruise/en/020/020/060/ev_yamagata.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.pa.thr.mlit.go.jp}}</ref> the priests of the shrine come from that family.<ref name=":1" />
== History == The bugaku costumes used in the Hayashike Bugaku performances are distinctively designed with bright crimson cloth, dyed using the benizome method. The surrounding Rokku district was historically inhabited by six families whose members performed alongside the Hayashi family. Descendants of these families continue to be responsible for Hayashike Bugaku performances to this day.<ref name=":0" /> The traditional dance is done on September 14-15 every year.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Benibana Museum (The Safflower Museum) |url=https://www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp/database/benibana/bunken/book/bmuseum/bmuseum3.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=www.lib.yamagata-u.ac.jp}}</ref>
== festivals == The main festival of the shrine is Donga Matsuri, which is the largest festival in the region. It features a procession with a portable shrine extending up to 2 km in length. Additionally, the festival showcases Yachi Yakko performers and Hayashi Yatai floats with musicians playing festive tunes. An essential part of the Donga Matsuri is the Yayu no bugaku, performed around bonfires at night, providing participants with a solemn atmosphere and a reflection on the eternal flow of time.<ref name=":0" />
Throughout the year, the shrine hosts various other seasonal events. These include the New Year’s Day Festival and Hatsumode on January 1, the Kiuri Tenno-sai and Chigaya-nowa-kuguri festivals in summer, and the Shichi-Go-San festival in autumn. These events hold significant spiritual importance for the local community.<ref name=":0" />
== References == {{reflist}}
